title: solidago virgaurea subsp. macrorhiza
intro: Solidago virgaurea subsp. macrorhiza, also known as tall goldenrod, is a perennial plant in the aster family that is native to North America. This plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable medicinal herb. The medicinal properties of Solidago virgaurea subsp. macrorhiza have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including arthritis, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, and may also help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and alleviate symptoms of allergies. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water or a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin.
